g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about RedBoot, a powerful embedded bootloader and debugger for microcontroller-based systems.
g.text: Red Hat RedBoot - Embedded Bootloader and Debugger

g.data-narration: RedBoot is a bootloader and debugger built on eCos hardware abstraction layer, designed for embedded systems and microcontrollers.
g.text: A bootloader and debugger for embedded systems developed by Red Hat,
built on the eCos HAL (Hardware Abstraction Layer).
Provides boot management, flash programming, network debugging, and CLI.

g.data-narration: RedBoot provides essential capabilities for embedded development: network booting, flash management, GDB debugging, and command-line interface.
g.text: Why RedBoot Matters in Embedded Systems
• First software to run when microcontroller powers on
• Initializes hardware, loads OS/application from memory
• Extends capabilities: network debugging, flash management, diagnostics

g.data-narration: RedBoot has four core components: Boot Loader Core for initialization, Flash Manager for memory operations, Network Stack for communication, and Command Interpreter.
The boot sequence follows six stages: Power-On Reset, Hardware Initialization, Memory Test, Environment Setup, Boot Script Execution, and Application Loading.
RedBoot manages flash memory using FIS (Flash Image System), which maintains a directory of images with their addresses, sizes, and names.
g.text: Flash Memory Management - FIS (Flash Image System)

g.data-narration: RedBoot supports DHCP for auto IP config, TFTP for network boot/firmware updates, and GDB stub for remote source-level debugging.
Essential RedBoot commands include: fis init/list/write for flash, load for TFTP, go for execution, and fconfig for configuration.
This example shows flashing an application: connect to RedBoot, fis init, configure network, load via TFTP, write to flash, and boot.
g.text: RedBoot> fis init - Initialize flash image system
RedBoot> load -r -b 0x100000 -m tftp app.bin - Load via TFTP
RedBoot> fis write -b 0x100000 -l 0x20000 -f 0x80000 - Write to flash
RedBoot> fis load application - Load from flash

g.data-narration: GDB stub enables remote debugging: activate stub in RedBoot, connect GDB from host, set breakpoints, examine variables, and step through code.
g.text: GDB Commands: break main, continue, print variable, step, next

g.data-narration: Boot scripts automate boot process with fallback logic: configure with fconfig, define script with echo/load/if statements, enable auto-run at boot.
g.text: Run script at boot: true - Enable auto-execution
Script: load -m tftp 192.168.1.10:kernel.bin - Network boot command

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: RedBoot is an eCos-based bootloader for embedded systems, supports flash management via FIS, network boot via TFTP, and remote debugging via GDB stub.
g.text: RedBoot: eCos-based bootloader/debugger for embedded systems
Boot sequence: Reset → Init → Memory → Env → Script → Load → Go
FIS: Flash Image System manages partitions (RedBoot, Config, App, Backup)
Network: DHCP, TFTP boot, GDB stub for debugging
